IERC20","name":"tokenOut","type":"address"},{"internalType":"address","name":"recipient","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"maxAmountIn","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"minAmountOut","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"deadline","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"unwrap","type":"bool"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"permitNonce","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"sigDeadline","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"signature","type":"bytes"}],"name":"swapPermit2","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amountIn","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amountOut","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"fee","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"stateMutability":"payable","type":"receive"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Enables EIP-7730 clear-signing metadata: wallet can display human-readable token names      because token addresses appear directly in calldata rather than as numeric indices.      Funding modes supported:      1. APPROVAL via the pool's callback funding \u2014 user approves THIS contract once per token.      2. Native ETH \u2014 user sends msg.value; the callback wraps to the pool's wrapper token.      3. Permit2 SignatureTransfer \u2014 user signs an EIP-712 witness keyed on token addresses;         no prior allowance to the Concierge needed (only Permit2's universal approval).      Security model:      - Callback context (cbUser, cbPool, cbMode, cbEthBudget) is stored in transient storage        (EIP-1153). cbPool doubles as the reentrancy guard: nonzero means a call is in flight.      - The callback validates msg.sender == cbPool before pulling any tokens, preventing        a malicious contract from hijacking the callback to drain user funds.      - Native wrap is gated on cbEthBudget (a snapshot of the entry-point msg.value), so        pre-stuck ETH cannot be silently consumed as a user's swap input. Any residual        balance is refunded by sweepEth to msg.sender after the body runs.      Mint queue (mint / swapMint with useQueue=true, or the *WithQueuePermit2Allowance      entry points):      - Self-contained per-pool FIFO. Funds stay in the user's wallet; each keeper-driven        execution pulls from the requester via their ERC20 approval to the Concierge OR, for        the *WithQueuePermit2Allowance entry points, via a Permit2 AllowanceTransfer allowance        registered once at enqueue (`PERMIT2.transferFrom` against a standing allowance \u2014 the        per-request `usePermit2Allowance` flag selects which). The pool itself is unmodified \u2014        queued executions go through the same callback machinery as direct calls, with        `_cbUser` pointing at the original requester instead of msg.sender.      - Try-first semantics: a user submitting with `useQueue=true` first attempts the mint        synchronously. A full fill returns normally and refunds the entire msg.value (no fee        is taken). A partial fill or recoverable revert (slippage, rate limit, \u03c3_swap gate)        enqueues the remainder; `NATIVE_KEEPER_FEE` is escrowed and the rest of msg.value        is refunded. Non-recoverable reverts bubble unchanged.      - Keeper economics: each execution that produces any fill pays the keeper a        `KEEPER_FEE_PPM` skim of input tokens (default 0.10%). The terminal-state keeper        (full fill, cancellation by deadline / insufficient funds / slippage timeout, or        sweeping a user-cancellation tombstone) also collects the request's native escrow.","errors":{"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"burn(address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"LP token amount to burn","minAmountsOut":"Per-token slippage floor on the payout. Zero means \"no floor\"                     for that slot. Length must equal numTokens.","pool":"PartyPool to burn from","recipient":"Address that receives the basket tokens"}},"burnPermit2(address,address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"LP token amount to burn","minAmountsOut":"Per-token MEV/slippage floor on the payout. Zero means \"no floor\"                      for that slot. Length must equal numTokens.","payer":"Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)","pool":"PartyPool to burn from","recipient":"Address that receives the basket tokens","unwrap":"If true and the native wrapper is in the basket, unwrap to ETH."}},"burnSwap(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"LP token amount to burn","pool":"PartyPool to burn from","recipient":"Address that receives the output tokens","tokenOut":"Address of the token to receive, or NATIVE for ETH (forces unwrap)"}},"burnSwapPermit2(address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"LP token amount to burn","minAmountOut":"Minimum output the user accepts (MEV/slippage floor)","payer":"Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)","pool":"PartyPool to burn from","recipient":"Address that receives the output tokens","tokenOut":"Address of the token to receive, or NATIVE for ETH (forces unwrap)","unwrap":"If true and tokenOut is the wrapper, unwrap to ETH (NATIVE forces it)"}},"liquidityPartySwapCallback(bytes32,address,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"Pool calls this for each input token it needs. The Concierge funds the pool using      one of four paths, selected by transient mode + per-call balance:        1. Native wrap: token == pool.immutables().wrapper and cbEthBudget covers it.        2. Permit2 (single): cbMode == PERMIT2 \u2014 pull from _cbUser via single permit.        3. Prepaid: cbMode == PREPAID \u2014 Concierge already holds the tokens (pulled via           a batch Permit2 in the entry point); just transfer to the pool.        4. Default: safeTransferFrom from _cbUser, using their Concierge allowance."},"mint(address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"lpTokenAmount":"Desired LP token amount to mint (upper bound)","maxAmountsIn":"Per-token MEV/slippage cap on the pool's deposit draw.                           Zero means \"no cap\" for that slot. Length must equal                           numTokens. When `useQueue=true`, each keeper-driven                           execution reserves headroom for the `KEEPER_FEE_PPM`                           skim by capping the pool's per-token draw at                           `maxAmountsIn[i] * 1e6 / (1e6 + KEEPER_FEE_PPM)`                           (further tightened by balance and allowance). The                           keeper fee is then skimmed from the requester's                           remaining headroom, so total per-token spend stays                           bounded by `maxAmountsIn[i]`. For the common case                           (max-uint approvals + ample balance) the immediate                           and queued paths agree to within rounding.","minLpOut":"Minimum LP issued; reverts \"slippage control\" otherwise.","partialFillAllowed":"When false, reverts \"rate limited\" if the per-window \u03b3 cap                           would force a partial fill.","pool":"PartyPool to mint into","recipient":"Address that receives the LP tokens","useQueue":"When true, the request enters the mint queue after a try-first                           attempt. Requires `partialFillAllowed=true` and no NATIVE in                           the basket. `msg.value` must be at least `NATIVE_KEEPER_FEE`;                           the deposit is refunded on a full fill or escrowed for the                           keeper on a partial / requeue."}},"mintPermit2(address,address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"The Concierge pulls each basket token at exactly `maxAmountsIn[i]` upfront, then      calls `pool.mint` with callback funding so the pool can withdraw only the actual      proportional deposit it computes. Any leftover token balance from the cap is      refunded to `payer` at the end.","params":{"lpTokenAmount":"Desired LP token amount (upper bound)","maxAmountsIn":"Per-token MEV/slippage caps; doubles as the Permit2 pull ceiling.                     Length must equal numTokens.","payer":"Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)","pool":"PartyPool to mint into","recipient":"Address that receives the LP tokens"}},"swap(address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"pool":"PartyPool to trade in","recipient":"Address that receives the output tokens","tokenIn":"Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH","tokenOut":"Address of the output token, or NATIVE to receive ETH (forces unwrap)"}},"swapMint(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"lpAmountOut":"Target LP shares to mint (partial-fill aware)","maxAmountIn":"MEV/slippage cap on the requester's total tokenIn                           spend (pool draw + keeper-fee skim) per fill. When                           `useQueue=true`, the pool's per-execution draw is                           capped at `maxAmountIn * 1e6 / (1e6 + KEEPER_FEE_PPM)`                           (further tightened by balance and allowance) so the                           keeper fee fits within `maxAmountIn`. See `mint` for                           the analogous rationale.","minLpOut":"Minimum LP issued; reverts \"slippage control\" otherwise.","partialFillAllowed":"When false, reverts \"rate limited\" on a capped fill.","pool":"PartyPool to mint into","recipient":"Address that receives the LP tokens","tokenIn":"Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH","useQueue":"When true, the request enters the mint queue after a try-first                           attempt. Requires `partialFillAllowed=true`. `tokenIn` may not                           be NATIVE \u2014 the user must approve the wrapper directly. See                           `mint` for native-fee semantics."}},"swapMintPermit2(address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"`tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20. `msg.value` is forbidden."},"swapPermit2(address,address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"`tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20 (Permit2 does not handle native ETH). `tokenOut`      may be NATIVE \u2014 that forces unwrap=true and the witness binds to it.      `msg.value` is forbidden on the Permit2 path.","params":{"payer":"Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)","permitNonce":"Permit2 nonce the user signed","recipient":"Address that receives the output","sigDeadline":"Permit2 signature deadline","signature":"Permit2 65-byte signature"}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"MintQueued(uint256,address,address,address,bool,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a mint request is enqueued \u2014 either because the user's try-first         attempt only partially filled (the partial mint emits the pool's normal Mint /         SwapMint event in addition) or reverted with a recoverable reason (slippage,         rate limit, \u03c3_swap gate). Not emitted on a full try-first fill."},"MintRequestCanceled(uint256,address,uint8)":{"notice":"Emitted when a queued mint request is canceled. `reason` is one of:         0 = USER (user called cancelMintRequest),         1 = DEADLINE (request.deadline expired before completion),         2 = TIMEOUT (kept failing slippage for SLIPPAGE_TIMEOUT_BLOCKS),         3 = INSUFFICIENT (couldn't pull the requester's tokens \u2014 approval revoked,             balance depleted, etc.)."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"KEEPER_FEE_PPM()":{"notice":"Keeper-fee skim in PPM applied to input tokens of every keeper-driven execution         of a queued mint. Default at deployment: 500 (0.05%)."},"NATIVE()":{"notice":"Sentinel for native chain currency (ETH). When passed as tokenIn or tokenOut,         the Concierge substitutes the pool's wrapper token internally; EIP-7730 wallets         typically render this address as \"ETH\". Value: 0xeee\u2026eee (40 'e' nibbles), the         widely-used native-currency sentinel (1inch, 0x, etc)."},"NATIVE_KEEPER_FEE()":{"notice":"Native-coin fee escrowed at enqueue and paid to the keeper that produces a         terminal state for a queued request. Sized to cover keeper gas in the worst         supported case (proportional mint into N=20 pool) plus a small premium."},"PERMIT2()":{"notice":"The Permit2 contract used for SignatureTransfer-funded operations."},"SLIPPAGE_TIMEOUT_BLOCKS()":{"notice":"Maximum number of blocks a queued request can keep failing slippage controls         (slippage / rate limit / volatile market) before being canceled. Default 300."},"_skimKeeperFee(address,address,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Internal helper exposed externally so the queue executor can wrap         the post-pool keeper-fee `safeTransferFrom` in a try/catch. Lives on         the Concierge (not the library) because Solidity's try/catch needs an         external CALL target \u2014 the library's delegatecall self-call would         exit and re-enter the Concierge, defeating the reentrancy guard the         library is asserting across this skim. Routed through `this.` (i.e.,         `IConciergeSkimSelf(address(this))`) by the library, so `msg.sender`         is the Concierge itself when this runs."},"_skimKeeperFeePermit2(address,address,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Permit2-allowance variant of `_skimKeeperFee`, for queued requests funded via         `MODE_PERMIT2_ALLOWANCE`. The requester granted the Concierge a Permit2         allowance (not an ERC20 approval), so the skim draws via `PERMIT2.transferFrom`.         Same `msg.sender == address(this)` gate and try/catch-routing rationale as         `_skimKeeperFee`."},"burn(address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Proportional burn: redeem LP tokens for the basket.         User must approve this contract for the pool's LP token."},"burnPermit2(address,address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Permit2-funded proportional burn. The user signs a single Permit2 permit for         the pool's LP token plus an address-keyed witness committing to the slippage         floor on every basket token. The Concierge pulls the LP and forwards the burn."},"burnSwap(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Single-token burn: redeem LP tokens for one output token.         User must approve this contract for the pool's LP token."},"burnSwapPermit2(address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Permit2-funded single-token burn (burnSwap). The user signs a Permit2 permit         for the LP token plus an address-keyed witness committing to the output token,         minimum output, and unwrap preference."},"cancelMintRequest(uint256)":{"notice":"Cancel a queued mint request (requester-only). See         `IPartyConcierge.cancelMintRequest`."},"escrowedNativeFees()":{"notice":"Aggregate native escrow held on behalf of live queued requests + tombstones."},"executeMints(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Drain up to `maxCount` head-of-queue requests for `pool`. See         `IPartyConcierge.executeMints` for the full keeper-economics contract."},"getMintRequest(uint256)":{"notice":"Current lifecycle state and stored fields of queued mint request `requestId`,         so a client can poll the progress of a request it enqueued by ID. See         `MintRequestState` for the three states and `MintRequest` for the fields."},"info()":{"notice":"Stateless view/quoter helper. Used by the mint queue to compute the         sandwich-resistant reference input for keeper-driven swapMint tranches         (`swapMintAmounts`), so the Concierge need not re-implement LMSR pricing."},"isMintRequestLive(uint256)":{"notice":"Whether `requestId` is still live (not tombstoned, not yet executed)."},"mint(address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool,uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Proportional mint: deposit all basket tokens, receive LP tokens.         User must approve this contract for every token in the pool. If the wrapper         token is in the pool, msg.value can cover its required deposit (auto-wrapped)."},"mintPermit2(address,address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Permit2-funded proportional mint. The user signs a single batch Permit2 permit         covering every basket token (with per-token caps == `maxAmountsIn`), plus an         address-keyed witness that binds all the mint parameters."},"mintWithQueuePermit2Allowance(address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,((address,uint160,uint48,uint48)[],address,uint256),bytes)":{"notice":"Queued proportional mint funded by Permit2 AllowanceTransfer. See         `IPartyConcierge.mintWithQueuePermit2Allowance`. The library registers the         allowance (`PERMIT2.permit`) and runs the try-first leg; keeper tranches draw         against the standing allowance."},"planner()":{"notice":"The PartyPlanner used to resolve token indices and validate pool support."},"queueLength(address)":{"notice":"Length of the (non-pruned) FIFO for a pool, including any live tombstones."},"swap(address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Swap tokenIn for tokenOut in pool. User must approve this contract for tokenIn         (or pass NATIVE + msg.value to pay with ETH)."},"swapMint(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Single-token mint: deposit one token, receive LP. params.minAmountsOut: Per-token slippage floor on the payout. Zero means "no floor"                     for that slot. Length must equal numTokens.
params.recipient: Address that receives the basket tokens

params.minAmountsOut: Per-token MEV/slippage floor on the payout. Zero means "no floor"                      for that slot. Length must equal numTokens.
params.payer: Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)
params.recipient: Address that receives the basket tokens
params.unwrap: If true and the native wrapper is in the basket, unwrap to ETH.

params.recipient: Address that receives the output tokens
params.tokenOut: Address of the token to receive, or NATIVE for ETH (forces unwrap)

params.minAmountOut: Minimum output the user accepts (MEV/slippage floor)
params.payer: Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)
params.recipient: Address that receives the output tokens
params.tokenOut: Address of the token to receive, or NATIVE for ETH (forces unwrap)
params.unwrap: If true and tokenOut is the wrapper, unwrap to ETH (NATIVE forces it)

liquidityPartySwapCallback(bytes32,addre.details: Pool calls this for each input token it needs. The Concierge funds the pool using      one of four paths, selected by transient mode + per-call balance:        1. Native wrap: token == pool.immutables().wrapper and cbEthBudget covers it.        2. Permit2 (single): cbMode == PERMIT2 — pull from _cbUser via single permit.        3. Prepaid: cbMode == PREPAID — Concierge already holds the tokens (pulled via           a batch Permit2 in the entry point); just transfer to the pool.        4. Default: safeTransferFrom from _cbUser, using their Concierge allowance.

params.lpTokenAmount: Desired LP token amount to mint (upper bound)
params.maxAmountsIn: Per-token MEV/slippage cap on the pool's deposit draw.                           Zero means "no cap" for that slot. Length must equal                           numTokens. When `useQueue=true`, each keeper-driven                           execution reserves headroom for the `KEEPER_FEE_PPM`                           skim by capping the pool's per-token draw at                           `maxAmountsIn[i] * 1e6 / (1e6 + KEEPER_FEE_PPM)`                           (further tightened by balance and allowance). The                           keeper fee is then skimmed from the requester's                           remaining headroom, so total per-token spend stays                           bounded by `maxAmountsIn[i]`. For the common case                           (max-uint approvals + ample balance) the immediate                           and queued paths agree to within rounding.
params.minLpOut: Minimum LP issued; reverts "slippage control" otherwise.
params.partialFillAllowed: When false, reverts "rate limited" if the per-window γ cap                           would force a partial fill.
params.recipient: Address that receives the LP tokens
params.useQueue: When true, the request enters the mint queue after a try-first                           attempt. Requires `partialFillAllowed=true` and no NATIVE in                           the basket. `msg.value` must be at least `NATIVE_KEEPER_FEE`;                           the deposit is refunded on a full fill or escrowed for the                           keeper on a partial / requeue.

mintPermit2(address,address,address,uint.details: The Concierge pulls each basket token at exactly `maxAmountsIn[i]` upfront, then      calls `pool.mint` with callback funding so the pool can withdraw only the actual      proportional deposit it computes. Any leftover token balance from the cap is      refunded to `payer` at the end.

params.maxAmountsIn: Per-token MEV/slippage caps; doubles as the Permit2 pull ceiling.                     Length must equal numTokens.
params.payer: Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)
params.recipient: Address that receives the LP tokens
Address that receives the output tokens
params.tokenIn: Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH
params.tokenOut: Address of the output token, or NATIVE to receive ETH (forces unwrap)

params.lpAmountOut: Target LP shares to mint (partial-fill aware)
params.maxAmountIn: MEV/slippage cap on the requester's total tokenIn                           spend (pool draw + keeper-fee skim) per fill. When                           `useQueue=true`, the pool's per-execution draw is                           capped at `maxAmountIn * 1e6 / (1e6 + KEEPER_FEE_PPM)`                           (further tightened by balance and allowance) so the                           keeper fee fits within `maxAmountIn`. See `mint` for                           the analogous rationale.
params.minLpOut: Minimum LP issued; reverts "slippage control" otherwise.
params.partialFillAllowed: When false, reverts "rate limited" on a capped fill.
params.recipient: Address that receives the LP tokens
params.tokenIn: Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH
params.useQueue: When true, the request enters the mint queue after a try-first                           attempt. Requires `partialFillAllowed=true`. `tokenIn` may not                           be NATIVE — the user must approve the wrapper directly. See                           `mint` for native-fee semantics.

swapMintPermit2(address,address,address,.details: `tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20. `msg.value` is forbidden.

swapPermit2(address,address,address,addr.details: `tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20 (Permit2 does not handle native ETH). `tokenOut`      may be NATIVE — that forces unwrap=true and the witness binds to it.      `msg.value` is forbidden on the Permit2 path.

params.payer: Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)

KEEPER_FEE_PPM().notice: Keeper-fee skim in PPM applied to input tokens of every keeper-driven execution         of a queued mint. Default at deployment: 500 (0.05%).

NATIVE().notice: Sentinel for native chain currency (ETH). When passed as tokenIn or tokenOut,         the Concierge substitutes the pool's wrapper token internally; EIP-7730 wallets         typically render this address as "ETH". Value: 0xeee…eee (40 'e' nibbles), the         widely-used native-currency sentinel (1inch, 0x, etc).

NATIVE_KEEPER_FEE().notice: Native-coin fee escrowed at enqueue and paid to the keeper that produces a         terminal state for a queued request. Sized to cover keeper gas in the worst         supported case (proportional mint into N=20 pool) plus a small premium.

PERMIT2().notice: The Permit2 contract used for SignatureTransfer-funded operations.

SLIPPAGE_TIMEOUT_BLOCKS().notice: Maximum number of blocks a queued request can keep failing slippage controls         (slippage / rate limit / volatile market) before being canceled. Default 300.

_skimKeeperFee(address,address,address,u.notice: Internal helper exposed externally so the queue executor can wrap         the post-pool keeper-fee `safeTransferFrom` in a try/catch. Lives on         the Concierge (not the library) because Solidity's try/catch needs an         external CALL target — the library's delegatecall self-call would         exit and re-enter the Concierge, defeating the reentrancy guard the         library is asserting across this skim. Routed through `this.` (i.e.,         `IConciergeSkimSelf(address(this))`) by the library, so `msg.sender`         is the Concierge itself when this runs.

_skimKeeperFeePermit2(address,address,ad.notice: Permit2-allowance variant of `_skimKeeperFee`, for queued requests funded via         `MODE_PERMIT2_ALLOWANCE`. The requester granted the Concierge a Permit2         allowance (not an ERC20 approval), so the skim draws via `PERMIT2.transferFrom`.         Same `msg.sender == address(this)` gate and try/catch-routing rationale as         `_skimKeeperFee`.

burn(address,address,uint256,uint256[],u.notice: Proportional burn: redeem LP tokens for the basket.         User must approve this contract for the pool's LP token.

burnPermit2(address,address,address,uint.notice: Permit2-funded proportional burn. The user signs a single Permit2 permit for         the pool's LP token plus an address-keyed witness committing to the slippage         floor on every basket token. The Concierge pulls the LP and forwards the burn.

burnSwap(address,address,address,uint256.notice: Single-token burn: redeem LP tokens for one output token.         User must approve this contract for the pool's LP token.

burnSwapPermit2(address,address,address,.notice: Permit2-funded single-token burn (burnSwap). The user signs a Permit2 permit         for the LP token plus an address-keyed witness committing to the output token,         minimum output, and unwrap preference.

cancelMintRequest(uint256).notice: Cancel a queued mint request (requester-only). See         `IPartyConcierge.cancelMintRequest`.

escrowedNativeFees().notice: Aggregate native escrow held on behalf of live queued requests + tombstones.

executeMints(address,uint256).notice: Drain up to `maxCount` head-of-queue requests for `pool`. See         `IPartyConcierge.executeMints` for the full keeper-economics contract.

getMintRequest(uint256).notice: Current lifecycle state and stored fields of queued mint request `requestId`,         so a client can poll the progress of a request it enqueued by ID. See         `MintRequestState` for the three states and `MintRequest` for the fields.

info().notice: Stateless view/quoter helper. Used by the mint queue to compute the         sandwich-resistant reference input for keeper-driven swapMint tranches         (`swapMintAmounts`), so the Concierge need not re-implement LMSR pricing.

isMintRequestLive(uint256).notice: Whether `requestId` is still live (not tombstoned, not yet executed).

mint(address,address,uint256,uint256[],u.notice: Proportional mint: deposit all basket tokens, receive LP tokens.         User must approve this contract for every token in the pool. If the wrapper         token is in the pool, msg.value can cover its required deposit (auto-wrapped).

mintPermit2(address,address,address,uint.notice: Permit2-funded proportional mint. The user signs a single batch Permit2 permit         covering every basket token (with per-token caps == `maxAmountsIn`), plus an         address-keyed witness that binds all the mint parameters.

mintWithQueuePermit2Allowance(address,ad.notice: Queued proportional mint funded by Permit2 AllowanceTransfer. See         `IPartyConcierge.mintWithQueuePermit2Allowance`. The library registers the         allowance (`PERMIT2.permit`) and runs the try-first leg; keeper tranches draw         against the standing allowance.

planner().notice: The PartyPlanner used to resolve token indices and validate pool support.

queueLength(address).notice: Length of the (non-pruned) FIFO for a pool, including any live tombstones.

swap(address,address,address,address,uin.notice: Swap tokenIn for tokenOut in pool. User must approve this contract for tokenIn         (or pass NATIVE + msg.value to pay with ETH).

swapMint(address,address,address,uint256.notice: Single-token mint: deposit one token, receive LP.         User must approve this contract for tokenIn (or pass NATIVE + msg.value).

swapMintWithQueuePermit2Allowance(addres.notice: Queued single-token mint funded by Permit2 AllowanceTransfer. See         `IPartyConcierge.swapMintWithQueuePermit2Allowance`.

swapPermit2(address,address,address,addr.notice: Permit2-funded swap. Caller (relayer) need not equal `payer`; the Permit2         signature authorizes the transfer. The Concierge's address-keyed witness binds         every operation parameter so the relayer cannot tamper with the trade.
